tfw next week's work gets done for u. i find myself consistently grateful for the gift of open source.
this is a well-calibrated take. i say this applies quite fittingly probably up till 2030, beyond that, i reserve some naive optimism.

imagine how much catastrophic forgetting already occurs over the span of a pretraining+midtraining+posttraining run. the models we get are probably heavily lobotimized relative to the full scope of knowledge latent in their datasets. there's so much to fix.
many are eager to aggressively expand the datasets while overlooking the more efficient alternative of improving learning algorithms to squeeze out more out of the existng datasets

using the probability itself as a progress proxy is neat though
